title: Macaranga peltata
taxonomy: Macaranga peltata is classified within the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the spurge family. This family comprises approximately 300 genera and over 7,000 species of flowering plants. Macaranga peltata is further classified within the subfamily Acalyphoideae, which is one of the two subfamilies within the Euphorbiaceae family. The genus Macaranga itself is native to tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands, and is characterized by its diverse species and unique morphological features. The specific classification of Macaranga peltata is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Rosids, order Malpighiales, family Euphorbiaceae, subfamily Acalyphoideae, tribe Acalyptoideae, genus Macaranga, species M. peltata.
uses: Macaranga peltata has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help in treating wounds and infections. In some parts of Asia, Macaranga peltata is used to treat fever and other respiratory issues. The leaves of the plant are also used in traditional medicine to treat digestive problems and skin conditions. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Macaranga peltata for medicinal purposes.

constituents: Macaranga peltata is rich in various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids. These active constituents contribute to its medicinal properties. Flavonoids present in Macaranga peltata are known to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant also contains saponins, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antihyperlipidemic effects. The bioactive compounds in Macaranga peltata have been studied for their potential health benefits.

intro: Macaranga peltata, also known as Peltanthe or Macaranga, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it useful in treating various conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Macaranga peltata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Macaranga peltata is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory and skin-related ailments.
